articles.title: Putin denounces Nato at scaled back Victory Day parade
articles.summary: The Russian leader used his annual speech to justify his so-called special military operation in Ukraine.

articles.title: International cyber attack disrupts swathe of universities and schools
articles.summary: A hacking group breached the academic software Canvas, used by thousands of schools and universities across the globe.

articles.title: PCPJack Credential Stealer Exploits 5 CVEs to Spread Worm-Like Across Cloud Systems
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new credential theft framework dubbed PCPJack that targets exposed cloud infrastructure and ousts any artifacts linked to TeamPCP from the environments. articles.title: Greece examines mystery naval drone found in Ionian Sea
articles.summary: Greek media say the drone, which reportedly had explosives, could be either Ukrainian or linked to the Russia-Ukraine war.

articles.title: Fake Call History Apps Stole Payments From Users After 7.3 Million Play Store Downloads
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered fraudulent apps on the official Google Play Store for Android that falsely claimed to offer access to call histories for any phone number, only to trick users into joining a subscription that provided fake data and incurred financial loss. articles.title: One Click, Total Shutdown: The "Patient Zero" Webinar on Killing Stealth Breaches
articles.summary: The hardest part of cybersecurity isn't the technology, it’s the people. Every major breach you’ve read about lately usually starts the same way: one employee, one clever email, and one "Patient Zero" infection. articles.title: PAN-OS RCE Exploit Under Active Use Enabling Root Access and Espionage
articles.summary: Palo Alto Networks has disclosed that threat actors may have attempted to unsuccessfully exploit a recently disclosed critical security flaw as early as April 9, 2026. articles.title: Ukraine: Over 3,000 attacks on healthcare since full-scale Russian invasion
articles.summary: The World Health Organization (WHO) has verified more than 3,000 attacks on healthcare in Ukraine since Russia launched its full-scale invasion in February 2022, the UN agency reported on Friday.

articles.title: Iran accuses US of 'reckless military adventure'
articles.summary: Abbas Araghchi says the US attacks each time there is a diplomatic solution on the table.

articles.title: Australian by-election a litmus test for right-wing One Nation Party
articles.summary: Victory for the populist party in Farrer, New South Wales, would see One Nation's first MP elected to Australia's national parliament.

articles.title: Oil prices rise after US and Iran exchange fire in Hormuz strait
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump says the ceasefire between Washington and Tehran is still in place.

articles.title: South Africa president faces call to resign after court ruling
articles.summary: The Constitutional Court says MPs were was wrong to block impeachment proceedings against Cyril Ramaphosa.

articles.title: Central African Republic: Funding cuts put humanitarian support at risk
articles.summary: The Central African Republic (CAR) is making progress towards stability and security but major aid budget cuts threaten humanitarian operations there, a senior official with the UN aid coordination office OCHA said on Friday in New York.

articles.title: World News in Brief: Somalia drought response, Gaza and Ukraine aid updates, human rights abuses in Tunisia
articles.summary: In Somalia’s Puntland region, dried out watering holes, animal carcasses and old pots filled with ash have become part of the landscape as worsening drought conditions deepen a growing hunger crisis.

articles.title: NASA’s SpaceX 34th Commercial Resupply Mission Overview
articles.summary: NASA and SpaceX are targeting a mid-May launch to deliver scientific investigations, supplies, and equipment to the International Space Station. articles.title: Musk v. Altman week 2: OpenAI fires back, and Shivon Zilis reveals that Musk tried to poach Sam Altman

articles.title: New Linux PamDOORa Backdoor Uses PAM Modules to Steal SSH Credentials
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new Linux backdoor named PamDOORa that's being advertised on the Rehub Russian cybercrime forum for $1,600 by a threat actor called "darkworm." The backdoor is designed as a Pluggable Authentication Module (PAM)-based post-exploitation toolkit that enables persistent SSH access by means of a magic password and specific TCP port combination.

articles.title: Ivanti EPMM CVE-2026-6973 RCE Under Active Exploitation Grants Admin-Level Access
articles.summary: Ivanti is warning that a new security flaw impacting Endpoint Manager Mobile (EPMM) has been explored in limited attacks in the wild. The high-severity vulnerability, CVE-2026-6973 (CVSS score: 7.2), is a case of improper input validation affecting EPMM before versions 12.6.1.1, 12.7.0.1, and 12.8.0.1. It allows "a remotely authenticated user with administrative access to achieve remote code

articles.title: Anger and resignation in Tenerife as hantavirus ship approaches
articles.summary: Some on the Spanish island worry the imminent arrival of the MV Hondius could pose a health risk.

articles.title: White House calls Mark Hamill 'sick' after actor's Trump grave post
articles.summary: The Star Wars actor apologised and deleted the post, which contained an AI image of the president in a shallow grave.

articles.title: Lebanon war leaves families foraging for food despite ceasefire
articles.summary: The fragile ceasefire in Lebanon hasn’t prevented “ongoing killing and displacement”, with villages in the south of the country rendered completely unrecognizable after Israeli strikes, aid teams reported on Friday.

articles.title: Bahrain and US float Security Council resolution on the Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: Bahrain and the United States have circulated a draft Security Council resolution calling for Iran to cease attacks in the Strait of Hormuz, their ambassadors outlined to journalists at UN Headquarters in New York on Thursday.

articles.title: NASA, Industry Advance High Performance Spaceflight Computing
articles.summary: For decades, NASA has advanced on-board spacecraft computer processors that coordinate and execute the functions needed to support mission success. Space computing originated in the 1960s with the Apollo Guidance Computers, which were pivotal for guidance, navigation, and control computations during NASA’s first Moon missions. articles.title: Glowing Views from the Space Station
articles.summary: NASA astronaut Chris Williams captured the Milky Way rising above Earth’s atmospheric glow on April 13, 2026, while aboard a SpaceX Dragon docked to the International Space Station. This atmospheric glow is also called airglow. It occurs when atoms and molecules in the upper atmosphere, excited by sunlight, emit light to shed their excess energy. […]

articles.title: TCLBANKER Banking Trojan Targets Financial Platforms via WhatsApp and Outlook Worms
articles.summary: Threat hunters have flagged a previously undocumented Brazilian banking trojan dubbed TCLBANKER that's capable of targeting 59 banking, fintech, and cryptocurrency platforms. The activity is being tracked by Elastic Security Labs under the moniker REF3076. articles.summary: The dark secret of enterprise security operations is that defenders have quietly institutionalized the practice of not looking. articles.title: Hovering objects and flashing lights: what we learned from UFO documents released by the Pentagon
articles.summary: The US has published transcripts, video clips and audio recordings about unidentified flying objects.

articles.title: World News in Brief: Haiti crisis continues, Gaza-West Bank update, Sir David Attenborough at 100
articles.summary: Haiti continues to face escalating violence and deepening insecurity, with gangs expanding their reach beyond Port-au-Prince and civilians increasingly caught between armed groups and security operations.

articles.title: ‘Victims need a voice, assistance and justice’: UN victims’ rights advocate calls for stronger action
articles.summary: The United Nations Victims’ Rights Advocate has called for stronger and sustained action on behalf of the people she serves across the UN system.

articles.title: Oil, plastics and climate: Why higher prices could speed a materials transition
articles.summary: As oil prices have risen amid geopolitical turmoil, one perhaps overlooked driver of climate change is coming into sharper focus: the production of plastics which are deeply tied to fossil fuels.

articles.title: NASA Names Brian Hughes to Launch Operations Role
articles.summary: NASA announced Friday that Brian Hughes will return to the agency as senior director of launch operations, based at the agency’s Kennedy Space Center in Florida. articles.title: NASA Welcomes Paraguay as 67th Artemis Accords Signatory

articles.title: Quasar Linux RAT Steals Developer Credentials for Software Supply Chain Compromise
articles.summary: A previously undocumented Linux implant codenamed Quasar Linux RAT (QLNX) is targeting developers' systems to establish a silent foothold as well as facilitate a broad range of post-compromise functionality, such as credential harvesting, keylogging, file manipulation, clipboard monitoring, and network tunneling. articles.title: Hungary's new PM to be sworn in during 'regime change' party
articles.summary: Nearly a month ago Péter Magyar steered his Tisza party to a landslide victory, sweeping away 16 years of rule by Viktor Orbán.

articles.title: Three dead after volcano erupts on Indonesian island
articles.summary: Officials had previously issued warnings on climbing the active volcano due to safety concerns.

articles.title: Electricity bills targeted in planned shakeup to energy pricing
articles.summary: The war in the Middle East has brought renewed attention to Britain's vulnerability to energy price shocks.

articles.title: Malaria vaccine is saving children’s lives: WHO
articles.summary: New evidence shows that malaria vaccination is significantly reducing child deaths in Africa and could have an even greater impact as programmes expand, the World Health Organization (WHO) said on Friday.

articles.title: Wider hantavirus outbreak risk is ‘absolutely low’, insists UN health agency
articles.summary: The risk of hantavirus spreading to the general population is “absolutely low”, the UN World Health Organization (WHO) stressed on Friday, as a flight attendant tested negative for the disease after coming into contact with an infected passenger from the cruise ship at the centre of the outbreak, who later died.
